access_finding.gate: Azure Front Door WAF (x-azure-ref header) returns HTTP 403 'Page Blocked by WAF' (411 bytes) to EVERY non-browser client — HTML app pages, /-/media/ blob PDFs, AND robots.txt alike

probes_blocked.result: 200 but returned HTML shell, not PDF bytes

access_finding.archive_org: web.archive.org unreachable from this sandbox (curl connection reset) AND WebFetch is policy-blocked from web.archive.org — Wayback route unavailable to the spec env
access_finding.route_that_worked: r.jina.ai reader (headless-browser engine) — passes the WAF because it renders as a real browser; returns EXTRACTED TEXT (markdown), not raw bytes. Used for structure + content + text-layer-quality signal only.
access_finding.consequence: Canonical raw PDF bytes (Bronze, invariant 2) are NOT obtainable from the spec-writer environment. Production fetch REQUIRES a browser-engine fetch seam (us_senate §2.5 precedent). Per-fixture raw-byte sha256 pins are therefore DEFERRED to the sampler/capture leg via that seam (see regime doc §7 pinning rule). No sha256 is guessed.
